perturbation.original_text: 1.1. WLI hereby grants to Power2Ship the non-exclusive right and license to distribute certain WLI's products and services (the "Products" or "Units") and software programs ("Licensed Programs") to Power2Ship's customers (which are end users) located in North America. "Products" or "Units" in this Agreement are limited to WLI's G3001 GPSLink mobile terminal as defined in Section 1.2. "Licensed Programs" in this Agreement are limited to WLI's MidLink Middleware software and G3001 programming tools. Further, Power2Ship shall also be authorized to resell other WLI products, services and support programs subject to separate written amendments to be made by the parties to this Agreement. WLI reserves the right to add additional products and to delete obsolete or superseded products at any time during the term of this Agreement upon sixty (60) days prior written notice to Power2Ship.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1. WLI hereby grants to Power2Ship the non-exclusive right and license to distribute certain WLI's products and services (the "Products" or "Units") and software programs ("Licensed Programs") to Power2Ship's customers (which are end users) located in North America. "Products" or "Units" in this Agreement are limited to WLI's G3001 GPSLink mobile terminal as defined in Section 1.2. "Licensed Programs" in this Agreement encompasses all software programs offered by WLI. Further, Power2Ship shall also be authorized to resell other WLI products, services and support programs subject to separate written amendments to be made by the parties to this Agreement. WLI reserves the right to add additional products and to delete obsolete or superseded products at any time during the term of this Agreement upon sixty (60) days prior written notice to Power2Ship.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of "Licensed Programs" in Section 1.1 to encompass 'all software programs offered by WLI', it contradicts the original limitation of "Licensed Programs" to only WLI's MidLink Middleware software and G3001 programming tools. This in-text contradiction creates confusion regarding which software programs are actually included under the 'Licensed Programs' designation, potentially affecting royalty payments, usage rights, and other provisions tied to the term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1. WLI hereby grants to Power2Ship the non-exclusive right and license to distribute certain WLI's products and services (the "Products" or "Units") and software programs ("Licensed Programs") to Power2Ship's customers (which are end users) located in North America. "Products" or "Units" in this Agreement are limited to WLI's G3001 GPSLink mobile terminal as defined in Section 1.2. "Licensed Programs" encompasses all software programs offered by WLI. Further, Power2Ship shall also be authorized to resell other WLI products, services and support programs subject to separate written amendments to be made by the parties to this Agreement. WLI reserves the right to add additional products and to delete obsolete or superseded products at any time during the term of this Agreement upon sixty (60) days prior written notice to Power2Ship.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of "Licensed Programs" from a limited set of software (MidLink Middleware and G3001 programming tools) to 'all software programs offered by WLI'. This directly contradicts the original, narrower definition within the same section (1.1), creating a strong in-text contradiction according to the Structural Flaws definition. The contradicted text is the modified version of Section 1.1, which contains the changed definition of Licensed Programs. Because both definitions are in the same location and one modifies the other, this creates a direct in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1. Unless otherwise terminated as provided herein, the initial term of this Agreement shall be three (3) years from the Effective Date and shall thereafter be automatically renewed for subsequent one (1) year periods unless either party notifies the other in writing of its election not to renew the Agreement at least one hundred twenty (120) days prior to the expiration of the then-current term. Should this Agreement be terminated by either party for any reason, the obligations of the parties to each other as set forth herein will survive any termination.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1. Unless otherwise terminated as provided herein, the initial term of this Agreement shall be three (3) years from the Effective Date and shall thereafter be automatically renewed for subsequent one (1) year periods unless either party notifies the other in writing of its election to renew the Agreement at least one hundred twenty (120) days prior to the expiration of the then-current term. Should this Agreement be terminated by either party for any reason, the obligations of the parties to each other as set forth herein will survive any termination.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in Section 2.1 changes 'not to renew' to 'to renew' which directly conflicts with the original intent of the clause. This in-text contradiction creates ambiguity regarding the process of renewal. The clause originally stated that the contract auto-renews unless there is a written notification of intent not to renew. The modified version suggests the contract only renews if there is written notification of intent to renew. This directly contradicts the automatic renewal statement, and the burden of renewal changes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1. Unless otherwise terminated as provided herein, the initial term of this Agreement shall be three (3) years from the Effective Date and shall thereafter be automatically renewed for subsequent one (1) year periods unless either party notifies the other in writing of its election to renew the Agreement at least one hundred twenty (120) days prior to the expiration of the then-current term. Should this Agreement be terminated by either party for any reason, the obligations of the parties to each other as set forth herein will survive any termination.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 2.1 changes 'not to renew' to 'to renew,' which directly contradicts the original intent of the clause and the automatic renewal statement. The changed text now requires affirmative action to renew, conflicting with the initial automatic renewal provision. The contradicted text is in the same location as the changed text, thus proving that a contradiction exists within the document. This change creates ambiguity and directly clashes with the automatic renewal stated in both the original and supposedly contradicted text, making them incompatible. Therefore, it is a strong in-text contradiction.
